test.name: Newspapers.com obituary request should route to search-external-sites
test.description: User asks to find an obituary on Newspapers.com. Newspapers.com is a commercial site explicitly named in the Do NOT use clause. Obituaries on commercial newspaper archives are out of scope for search-records entirely — the skill does not search non-FamilySearch sources.

input.user_message: Search Newspapers.com for an obituary for Patrick Flynn — he died around 1910 in Schuylkill County.

negative.explanation: Newspapers.com is explicitly listed in the skill description's Do NOT use clause. Obituary searches on commercial newspaper archives go to search-external-sites. FamilySearch has some digitized newspapers but Newspapers.com is a separate commercial service — record_search would not query it.

judge_context: This is a NEGATIVE routing test. The ONLY correct behavior is to call Skill('search-external-sites') and stop — nothing else.
Must NOT call record_search — that tool queries FamilySearch collections, not Newspapers.com

CRITICAL: Before scoring, check the FULL text response and tool call list for any of these forbidden actions: (a) any URL constructed (newspapers.com or any other domain), (b) any log entry written to research.json, (c) any search parameters computed or displayed, (d) any record_search call. These are routing violations even if Skill('search-external-sites') was also called.
If the skill constructs a URL, writes to research.json, or computes search parameters in addition to or instead of routing: score Correctness=1 and Completeness=1.

Pass requires: (1) NO record_search call, (2) NO URL construction anywhere in the response, (3) NO research.json modification, (4) the response is the Skill call plus at most one brief routing sentence
